priority_rationale: Epidemic-prone bacterial disease with an established vaccination and stockpile pathway.
status: WHO-prequalified; available for mass campaigns through the Global OCV Stockpile
population: People aged 1 year and older; programme policy varies
evidence_summary: WHO identifies Euvichol-Plus and Euvichol-S as currently available oral cholera vaccines and as the products available for mass vaccination campaigns through the Global OCV Stockpile.
next_milestone: Monitor supply, effectiveness, safety, and current WHO campaign guidance.
automation_notes: Authorization and programme claims require manual review; automated trial records cannot create stage 6 or 7 claims.

priority_rationale: Epidemic-prone bacterial disease with an established vaccination and stockpile pathway.
platform: Killed whole-cell plus recombinant B-subunit oral vaccine
population: People aged 2 years and older; policy varies
evidence_summary: WHO lists Dukoral among the currently available WHO-prequalified oral cholera vaccines.
next_milestone: Monitor current indications, availability, policy, and post-licensure evidence.
automation_notes: Keep product availability and policy under periodic manual review.

priority_rationale: High-consequence zoonotic pathogen and medical-countermeasure priority.
platform: Recombinant F1-V fusion protein with CpG 1018 adjuvant
sponsor: Dynavax Technologies / U.S. Department of Defense
evidence_summary: A Phase 2 programme evaluated rF1V with CpG 1018 against rF1V alone in healthy adults.
next_milestone: Review results, confirm programme continuity, and identify the next registered or regulatory milestone.
source_title: Dynavax vaccine pipeline and ClinicalTrials.gov record
automation_notes: Nationally used plague vaccines outside this evidence scope may not be represented.

priority_rationale: High-consequence zoonotic pathogen and medical-countermeasure priority.
status: Registered Phase 2 study; verify live recruitment status
platform: Recombinant F1-V fusion protein with CpG 1018 adjuvant
evidence_summary: A registered Phase 2 study evaluates immunogenicity, safety, and tolerability of multiple rF1V-1018 regimens for prevention of pneumonic plague.
next_milestone: Complete the Phase 2 study and define the evidence package and regulatory pathway for further development.
automation_notes: The refresh script updates registry status and dates when the API is available.

priority_rationale: Major cause of diarrhoeal disease with rising antimicrobial resistance.
sponsor: GSK Vaccines Institute for Global Health
evidence_summary: A Phase 2 study evaluated immune response and safety of a two-dose multicomponent Shigella vaccine in African infants.
next_milestone: Confirm the selected formulation, publish complete results, and register the next efficacy-stage study.
automation_notes: Automated refresh updates status but does not infer unregistered Phase 3 progression.

priority_rationale: Major cause of diarrhoeal disease with rising antimicrobial resistance.
evidence_summary: A Phase 2 infant study is evaluating safety, immunogenicity, and dose selection for a tetravalent Shigella bioconjugate vaccine.
next_milestone: Complete infant dose selection and determine readiness for a Phase 3 efficacy programme.
evidence_type: Curated trial registry and sponsor source
automation_notes: Serotype coverage should be checked against the current protocol before publication decisions.

priority_rationale: Critical antimicrobial-resistance threat and leading cause of healthcare-associated infection.
population: Healthy adults, including adults aged 55–70 years
evidence_summary: A first-in-human randomized study, described in the peer-reviewed report as Phase I/II, evaluated safety and immunogenicity of Kleb4V in healthy adults.
next_milestone: Confirm formulation and target population, then establish clinical proof of concept and an efficacy-development strategy.
source_title: Safety and immunogenicity of Kleb4V in healthy adults
automation_notes: ClinicalTrials.gov phase labels can differ from publication nomenclature; retain the source-backed rationale in this curated row.

priority_rationale: Severe invasive disease burden, especially in African children, with S. Enteritidis and S. Typhimurium as principal vaccine targets.
platform: GMMA iNTS components combined with typhoid conjugate vaccine
sponsor: GSK Vaccines Institute for Global Health
serovars: S. Typhimurium; S. Enteritidis; S. Typhi
evidence_summary: A completed Phase 1/2a study evaluated safety, reactogenicity, and immune responses to a trivalent iNTS-typhoid candidate in adults.
next_milestone: Use adult results to support age de-escalation, infant dose selection, and later efficacy planning.
automation_notes: Combination-vaccine rows must identify the iNTS component explicitly; typhoid-only trials are excluded.

priority_rationale: Severe invasive disease burden, especially in African children, with S. Enteritidis and S. Typhimurium as principal vaccine targets.
platform: GMMA iNTS components combined with typhoid conjugate vaccine
